taskDescription: Click on the anatomical structures that are specifically found at the T8 vertebral level on a CT cross-section (AN51.1).
textField: At the T8 level, the *IVC* pierces the central tendon of the diaphragm through the caval foramen. The *hepatic* veins drain into the IVC just below this level. The dome of the right lobe of the *liver* is present at T8. The oesophagus is visible in the posterior mediastinum but does not pierce the diaphragm until T10. The descending thoracic *aorta* lies slightly left of midline posterior to the oesophagus. The pylorus of the stomach is not at this level as it lies at L1. The *heart* chambers, specifically the ventricles, are still visible at T8. The kidneys are not present at T8 as they lie at the L1-L2 level. The fundus of the *spleen* begins to appear at the T8-T9 level on the left side.
